records.m_pText: Three Passages to Central Command
Let me brief you on the three$npassages that link to Central$nCommand.
Er, right. We’re listening.$nJust…go on.
Three paths connect Central$nCommand to Eternia proper,$neach of them different.
The central passage is the shortest.$nBUT, the enemies we’ll find there$nare all nightmares.
The western passage is longer$nand the enemies tough, but not$nas tough as on the center path.
None of the guards along the$neast path are very strong, but$nthe distance is extremely long.
There are no easy options here,$nbut the choice is ours.$nWhat do we do?
Let’s Hurry
What’s wrong, [PCF1]?$nWhy did you stop?
Wait, [PCF2].$n[PCF1] needs a moment…
But we’re almost to the Earth Crystal.$nWe should hurry and awaken it!
Everlast Tower is just ahead once we$npush through Central Command!
[PCF2], my heart aches…$nI know what this must be like for you.$nI am sorry…
[PCF1]…
[PCF1], hurry! If we don’t$nawaken that crystal,$nthe whole world will get$nswallowed up in darkness!
We didn’t come this far$njust to stop now, did we?
There’s only one to go!$nAwaken the Earth Crystal$nand it’s all over!
Let’s go, [PCF1]. Together.$nThe world is waiting for us$nto awaken this crystal.
I know I must go…$nI know, but—
Right!$nEveryone else in?
…I’ll go.
Of course we’re coming.
Four Birds in a Cage
[PCF1]! [PCF2]!$n[PCM2]! Are you alright!?
Fit as a fiddle.
I am unharmed as well!
Augh, rude!$nWhat’s the big idea, leaving me out!?
Ahh, sorry!
…Wait!$n[PCF2] isn’t here!
What!? Where is she, then!?
Think about it. This is her home.$nShe has friends here. Family.$nRight about now, she—
You can’t think this was$nall a trap!?
[PCF2] is not the sort of$nperson to commit such deception.$nI will not believe it!
She’s right, me neither!
And me least of all!
I’m saying she’s in more danger$nthan all of us! We’re only$nenemies of the state…
What, and [PCF2] isn’t!?
With her, it’s personal! She$nbetrayed them, [PCM1]!$nPeople here must bear a grudge$nagainst her!
So you suspect she is being$nsubjected to an especially$ncruel punishment!?
[PCM2], your journal!$nWhat does it say? Anything about$ngetting out of here!?
I’ll have a look…
Rrgh, no luck!$nI suppose even miraculous tomes$nhave their limits.
Then let us send Airy as a scout.
What? Airy as a what, now?
You can pass between these bars.$nPlease, go and have a look$naround the area.
Nice thinking, [PCF1]!$nShe may be able to track down$nthe cell keys!
But…but what if someone sees me!?
Do you think I can just muscle$nthrough like you all? Do you see$nany muscle on me? At all!?
If we all remain here, we’ll get$nno closer to the Earth Crystal.
Or to [PCF2]! At least see if$nshe’s being held here in the$ndungeons or not!
Oh, fine…$nYou want me to go, I’ll go.$nWouldn’t want [PCF2] harmed…
Not that anyone cares what$nhappens to ME. *sniff*
Thank you, Airy. I shall be$npraying to my pendant for your$nsafe return.
I expect I’ll need it.$nAnyway, here goes nothing…
Shh! Wait! I hear something!
Eeek! What do I do!?$nSomeone’s coming!$nI’ve got to hide! Gotta hide!
What’s with all the noise?$nI could hear you guys from outside.
That voice—[PCF2]!
Sorry to keep you waiting!$nI found the keys to this place.
I’m just glad to see you’re safe!
Glad You’re Alright, [PCM1]
Thanks. I owe you one.
Hey, [PCM1]…
[PCM1], how do you feel$nabout [PCF1]?$nBe honest!
Wha— Whoa! Wh-where is this$ncoming from!?
Never mind.$nIt’s written on your face.
My face?$nAnd hey, keep it down, would you!?
Don’t worry, she can’t hear us$nfrom here.
And you can’t keep hiding your$nfeelings, anyway. Go tell her.
Tell her you like her.
What!? No, I don’t… Well, I mean,$nI… The crystals come first!$nThis is no time to be—
Heh, I didn’t mean right this second.$nSome time, when it’s just you two.
You tell her, take her hand,$nand walk your path together,$nside by side.
I mean it, [PCM1].$nYou’re not going to find anyone else$nlike her if you pass this up.
Not ever! Never ever ever!
Look, would you please keep it down!?
Glad You’re Alright, [PCM2]
Thank you, my dear. I owe you.
And a gentleman always pays his debts.$nDebts to ladies, I repay tenfold!
Ugh, here he goes.$nIt’s always this!
If you would have me do something$nelse, milady needs but ask.
Alright then, [PCM2],$nhow about you go out with me!?
Er, that’s very, um…$nM-might I ask what provoked that?
If you were my boyfriend,$nwhere would you take me?$nWhere is tonight’s date?
T-tonight!? Er, let me see.$nI need time to prepare a—
All this time, and you never$nonce stopped to think what to$ndo with me if I ever said yes!?
W-well, you see…
Pffft, ha ha ha!$nI’m only teasing you!
You can dish it out,$nbut you can’t take it at all!$nOh, this is useful information.
Wha—!?
You’ve been treating [PCM1]$nlike a little brother$nthis whole time.
And every time you get the chance,$nyou tease poor [PCF1].
So… Who do I tell this to first?
…Is neither an option?$nI vote neither.
Then you owe me twice the hush money!
Ah ha ha…
Let’s Press On
You’re staring at that journal$nfit to burn a hole in it.
I may as well. The thing’s$nstopped coughing up prophecies.
Just who was the author in the end?$nI can’t help but wonder…
Did someone in the past foretell$nwhat the four of us would do?
Or are the “us” in the book$nactually other people who$nlived in the past?
Other people whose doings perfectly$noverlap with our own?
Both theories are equally hard$nto believe.
According to the text, you’ve$nmet the book’s author before,$n[PCF1].
Any recollection?
No. I am not entirely without$nmy suspicions, but…$nI feel they must be wrong…
After all…you don’t recall$nwriting it, do you, [PCM2]?
Me? I’m afraid not.
And the author claims to have$nsaved your life, [PCM1].
Do you remember anything?
No, nothing. I’d always just$nassumed it was Owen that$nrescued me.
And [PCF2], the author claims$nhe’s madly in love with you.$nAny ideas? …So I can pummel him?
If I said no, I’d be lying.
What!?
I’m saying I have my suspicions$nwhere boys in love with me are$nconcerned.
But the facts don’t add up.$nFor either of them.
Either!? I suddenly find myself$ntwice as angry.
Regardless. To the extent its$nwords can still be trusted,$nthe book says…
It says you three are going to$nmeet the author if we keep$npressing on.
I see little point in fretting$nover someone we may or may not$nmeet.
The Earth Crystal awaits us.$nThis much is fact. So let us$nhurry to the Everlast Tower.
Seconded! Awakening the$ncrystals has to be our$ntop priority!
Then let us hurry on.
…Right…
